---
title: "Frostwire and firestarter"
date: 2008-01-03
forum: General Help
---

### Post by 1llusion on 2008-01-03
I've done some searching around, and cannot seem to find anything that helps me or works.  Frostwire will not connect to the network, it just sits there and says that it is trying, but never prevails.  I have attempted to use Firestarter to manage my firewall, but I find it difficult and confusing to do so.
I am at home trying this, with a wired connection.  limewire works for my brother on his windows machine, so I assume that the router is not the issue here.
I have read something about Policies regarding firestarter, but have not yet understood what their use is, or how to use them effectively.  
Qbittorrent works fine, if that is of any importance, as well as web browsers and pidgin.

I have tried replacing the gnutella.net file within .frostwire with the following, as instructed by a member on these boards in another thread, but that does not work.
```
uhc2.limewire.com:20181,,1162480626164,,,en,0
uhc.udp-host-cache.com:9999,,1162480630012,,,en,0
cache.kicks-***.net:8000,,1162480627277,,,en,1
```

### Post by AlexThomson_NZ on 2008-01-03
Frostwire is not having problems with IPv6 is it (the same way firefox does in Ubuntu when using a non IPv6 router)?  Does it work at all when firestarter is disabled?

---

### Post by 1llusion on 2008-01-03
I'm not sure about IPv6, how would I go about checking that?

I just tried starting Firestarter and stopping the firewall, then running Frostwire, but still no connection.
